lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਜਸੁ ਸਾਤਕੁ ਤਾਮਸੁ ਡਰਪਹਿ ਕੇਤੇ ਰੂਪ ਉਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: raajas saatak taamas ddarapeh kete roop upaaeaa |
lines.english: Those who embody the energies of sattva-white light, raajas-red passion, and taamas-black darkness, abide in the Fear of God, along with the many created forms.

lines.gurmukhi: ਛਲ ਬਪੁਰੀ ਇਹ ਕਉਲਾ ਡਰਪੈ ਅਤਿ ਡਰਪੈ ਧਰਮ ਰਾਇਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: chhal bapuree ih kaulaa ddarapai at ddarapai dharam raaeaa |3|
lines.english: This miserable deceiver Maya abides in the Fear of God; the Righteous Judge of Dharma is utterly afraid of Him as well. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਭਗਤਨ ਕਾ ਸੰਗੀ ਭਗਤ ਸੋਹਹਿ ਦਰਬਾਰਾ ॥੪॥੧॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak bhagatan kaa sangee bhagat soheh darabaaraa |4|1|
lines.english: Says Nanak, God is the companion of His devotees; His devotees look beautiful in the Court of the Lord. ||4||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲ ਸਮਗ੍ਰੀ ਡਰਹਿ ਬਿਆਪੀ ਬਿਨੁ ਡਰ ਕਰਣੈਹਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: sagal samagree ddareh biaapee bin ddar karanaihaaraa |
lines.english: The entire expanse of the Universe is in the Fear of God; only the Creator Lord is without this Fear.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਂਚ ਬਰਖ ਕੋ ਅਨਾਥੁ ਧ੍ਰੂ ਬਾਰਿਕੁ ਹਰਿ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਅਮਰ ਅਟਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: paanch barakh ko anaath dhraoo baarik har simarat amar attaare |
lines.english: The five year old orphan boy Dhroo, by meditating in remembrance on the Lord, became stationary and permanent.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Maaroo, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਤ੍ਰ ਹੇਤਿ ਨਾਰਾਇਣੁ ਕਹਿਓ ਜਮਕੰਕਰ ਮਾਰਿ ਬਿਦਾਰੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: putr het naaraaein kahio jamakankar maar bidaare |1|
lines.english: For the sake of his son, Ajaamal called out, "O Lord, Naaraayan", who struck down and killed the Messenger of Death. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੇ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕੇਤੇ ਅਗਨਤ ਉਧਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: mere tthaakur kete aganat udhaare |
lines.english: My Lord and Master has saved many, countless beings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਲਮੀਕੁ ਸੁਪਚਾਰੋ ਤਰਿਓ ਬਧਿਕ ਤਰੇ ਬਿਚਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: baalameek supachaaro tario badhik tare bichaare |
lines.english: Baalmeek the outcaste was saved, and the poor hunter was saved as well.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਹਿ ਦੀਨ ਅਲਪ ਮਤਿ ਨਿਰਗੁਣ ਪਰਿਓ ਸਰਣਿ ਦੁਆਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: mohi deen alap mat niragun pario saran duaare |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I am meek, with little or no understanding, and unworthy; I seek protection at the Lord's Door. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੀਨੀ ਰਖਿਆ ਭਗਤ ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦੈ ਹਰਨਾਖਸ ਨਖਹਿ ਬਿਦਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: keenee rakhiaa bhagat prahilaadai haranaakhas nakheh bidaare |
lines.english: He saved His devotee Prahlaad, and tore Harnaakhash with his nails.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕ ਨਿਮਖ ਮਨ ਮਾਹਿ ਅਰਾਧਿਓ ਗਜਪਤਿ ਪਾਰਿ ਉਤਾਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: ek nimakh man maeh araadhio gajapat paar utaare |2|
lines.english: The elephant remembered the Lord in his mind for an instant, and so was carried across. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਨ ਪਰਾਧ ਬਤਾਵਉ ਅਪੁਨੇ ਮਿਥਿਆ ਮੋਹ ਮਗਨਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: kavan paraadh bataavau apune mithiaa moh maganaare |
lines.english: What sins of mine should I speak of? I am intoxicated with false emotional attachment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਦਰੁ ਦਾਸੀ ਸੁਤੁ ਭਇਓ ਪੁਨੀਤਾ ਸਗਲੇ ਕੁਲ ਉਜਾਰੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: bidar daasee sut bheo puneetaa sagale kul ujaare |3|
lines.english: Bidar, the son of a slave-girl, was purified, and all his generations were redeemed. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਇਓ ਸਾਮ ਨਾਨਕ ਓਟ ਹਰਿ ਕੀ ਲੀਜੈ ਭੁਜਾ ਪਸਾਰੇ ॥੪॥੨॥
lines.translit: aaeo saam naanak ott har kee leejai bhujaa pasaare |4|2|
lines.english: Nanak has entered the Sanctuary of the Lord; please, reach out and take me into Your embrace. ||4||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜੋ ਕਰਮ ਕੀਏ ਹਉ ਹਉਮੈ ਤੇ ਤੇ ਭਏ ਅਜਾਏ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jo jo karam kee hau haumai te te bhe ajaae |1|
lines.english: The deeds I did in egotism and pride, have all been done in vain. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਤ ਨਵਿਤ ਭ੍ਰਮਿਓ ਬਹੁ ਭਾਤੀ ਅਨਿਕ ਜਤਨ ਕਰਿ ਧਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: vit navit bhramio bahu bhaatee anik jatan kar dhaae |
lines.english: For the sake of riches, I wandered around in so many ways; I rushed around, making all sorts of efforts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Maaroo, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਦਿਨੁ ਮੋ ਕਉ ਦੀਜੈ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਜੀਉ ਜਾ ਦਿਨ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਗਾਏ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: so din mo kau deejai prabh jeeo jaa din har jas gaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: please bless me with those days, O Dear God, on which I may sing the Lord's Praises. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਵਰ ਦਿਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਕਾਜ ਨ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: avar din kaahoo kaaj na laae |
lines.english: Other days are of no use to me;

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਤ੍ਰ ਕਲਤ੍ਰ ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਦੇਖਿ ਪਸਾਰਾ ਇਸ ਹੀ ਮਹਿ ਉਰਝਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: putr kalatr grih dekh pasaaraa is hee meh urajhaae |
lines.english: Gazing upon children, spouse, household and possessions, one is entangled in these.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਮਦ ਚਾਖਿ ਭਏ ਉਦਮਾਤੇ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਕਬਹੁ ਨ ਗਾਏ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: maaeaa mad chaakh bhe udamaate har har kabahu na gaae |2|
lines.english: Tasting the wine of Maya, one is intoxicated, and never sings of the Lord, Har, Har. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹ ਬਿਧਿ ਖੋਜੀ ਬਹੁ ਪਰਕਾਰਾ ਬਿਨੁ ਸੰਤਨ ਨਹੀ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: eih bidh khojee bahu parakaaraa bin santan nahee paae |
lines.english: In this way, I have examined lots of methods, but without the Saints, it is not found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਆਗਿਓ ਸਗਲਾ ਮਾਨੁ ਮਹਤਾ ਦਾਸ ਰੇਣ ਸਰਣਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: tiaagio sagalaa maan mahataa daas ren saranaae |
lines.english: Abandoning all pride and self-importance, I have sought the Sanctuary of the dust of the feet of the Lord's slave.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਮ ਦਾਤਾਰ ਵਡੇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਸੰਮ੍ਰਥ ਮਾਗਨ ਕਉ ਦਾਨੁ ਆਏ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: tum daataar vadde prabh samrath maagan kau daan aae |3|
lines.english: You are the Great Giver, the great and almighty God; I have come to beg a gift from You. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Maaroo, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਨ ਥਾਨ ਧੀਰਿਓ ਹੈ ਨਾਮਾ ਕਵਨ ਬਸਤੁ ਅਹੰਕਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: kavan thaan dheerio hai naamaa kavan basat ahankaaraa |
lines.english: In what place is the Name established? Where does egotism dwell?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਭਏ ਏਕੈ ਮਹਾ ਅਨੰਦ ਸੁਖ ਪਾਏ ॥੪॥੩॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak har mil bhe ekai mahaa anand sukh paae |4|3|
lines.english: Says Nanak, meeting with the Lord, I have become one with Him; I have found supreme bliss and peace. ||4||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਨ ਚਿਹਨ ਸੁਨਿ ਊਪਰਿ ਛੋਹਿਓ ਮੁਖ ਤੇ ਸੁਨਿ ਕਰਿ ਗਾਰਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kavan chihan sun aoopar chhohio mukh te sun kar gaaraa |1|
lines.english: What injury have you suffered, listening to abuse from someone else's mouth? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਤੀ ਨ ਜਾਨਉ ਕੇਤੀਕ ਮੁਦਤਿ ਚਲਤੇ ਖਬਰਿ ਨ ਪਾਇਓ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: etee na jaanau keteek mudat chalate khabar na paaeo |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You don't even know how long you will stay here; you have no hint of when you shall leave. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਨਹੁ ਰੇ ਤੂ ਕਉਨੁ ਕਹਾ ਤੇ ਆਇਓ ॥
lines.translit: sunahu re too kaun kahaa te aaeo |
lines.english: Listen: who are you, and where did you come from?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਤਤ ਮਿਲਿ ਭਇਓ ਸੰਜੋਗਾ ਇਨ ਮਹਿ ਕਵਨ ਦੁਰਾਤੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: panch tat mil bheo sanjogaa in meh kavan duraate |2|
lines.english: The union of the five tattvas - the five elements - has brought you into being. Which of these is evil? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਨ ਸੀਲ ਪਵਨ ਅਰੁ ਪਾਣੀ ਬਸੁਧਾ ਖਿਮਾ ਨਿਭਰਾਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: sehan seel pavan ar paanee basudhaa khimaa nibharaate |
lines.english: Wind and water have patience and tolerance; the earth has compassion and forgiveness, no doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮ ਮਰਣੁ ਉਸ ਹੀ ਕਉ ਹੈ ਰੇ ਓਹਾ ਆਵੈ ਜਾਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: janam maran us hee kau hai re ohaa aavai jaaee |3|
lines.english: He alone is born and dies; He alone comes and goes. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਰਚਿ ਰਚਿਆ ਪੁਰਖਿ ਬਿਧਾਤੈ ਨਾਲੇ ਹਉਮੈ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jin rach rachiaa purakh bidhaatai naale haumai paaee |
lines.english: The Primal Lord, the Architect of Destiny, formed your form; He also burdened you with egotism.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਰਨੁ ਚਿਹਨੁ ਨਾਹੀ ਕਿਛੁ ਰਚਨਾ ਮਿਥਿਆ ਸਗਲ ਪਸਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: baran chihan naahee kichh rachanaa mithiaa sagal pasaaraa |
lines.english: Nothing of the color and the form of the creation shall remain; the entire expanse is transitory.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਣਤਿ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਜਬ ਖੇਲੁ ਉਝਾਰੈ ਤਬ ਏਕੈ ਏਕੰਕਾਰਾ ॥੪॥੪॥
lines.translit: bhanat naanak jab khel ujhaarai tab ekai ekankaaraa |4|4|
lines.english: Prays Nanak, when He brings His play to its close, then only the One, the One Lord remains. ||4||4||
